description: FORK_A can be held by at most one philosopher at a time (shared between PHILOSOPHER_A and PHILOSOPHER_B)
FORK_B can be held by at most one philosopher at a time (shared between PHILOSOPHER_B and PHILOSOPHER_C)
FORK_C can be held by at most one philosopher at a time (shared between PHILOSOPHER_C and PHILOSOPHER_D)
FORK_D can be held by at most one philosopher at a time (shared between PHILOSOPHER_D and PHILOSOPHER_E)
FORK_E can be held by at most one philosopher at a time (shared between PHILOSOPHER_E and PHILOSOPHER_F)
FORK_F can be held by at most one philosopher at a time (shared between PHILOSOPHER_F and PHILOSOPHER_G)
FORK_G can be held by at most one philosopher at a time (shared between PHILOSOPHER_G and PHILOSOPHER_A)
PHILOSOPHER_A needs FORK_A (left) and FORK_G (right) simultaneously to eat
PHILOSOPHER_B needs FORK_B (left) and FORK_A (right) simultaneously to eat
PHILOSOPHER_C needs FORK_C (left) and FORK_B (right) simultaneously to eat
PHILOSOPHER_D needs FORK_D (left) and FORK_C (right) simultaneously to eat
PHILOSOPHER_E needs FORK_E (left) and FORK_D (right) simultaneously to eat
PHILOSOPHER_F needs FORK_F (left) and FORK_E (right) simultaneously to eat
PHILOSOPHER_G needs FORK_G (left) and FORK_F (right) simultaneously to eat
Each philosopher must pick up both adjacent forks before eating and put down both forks after eating
Classic 7-way circular wait: each philosopher holds left fork and waits for right fork, forming a 7-node cycle
Partial circular wait: any subset of adjacent philosophers can form a shorter deadlock cycle
